They went on to start Character.AI that sameyear.", "relevance_score": 0.6}, {"title": "12 Hot Startups to Join Founded by Ex-Google Engineers", "url": "https://wellfound.com/job-collections/12-hot-startups-to-join-founded-by-ex-google-engineers", "snippet": "[DDG Snippet] What are some of the most interesting companies todayfoundedbyformerGoogleengineers? We found close to 200 recent startups in the AngelList database that were started by formerGoogleengineers.", "relevance_score": 0.6}, {"title": "20 Hot Startups Founded by Former Google Employees - Business Insider", "url": "https://www.businessinsider.com/20-enterprise-startups-former-google-employees-2019-6?op=1", "snippet": "[DDG Snippet] 20 of the hottest enterprise startups of 2019foundedbyformerGoogleemployees", "relevance_score": 0.6}, {"title": "Top Startups Founded by Ex Google Employees Shaping Tech", "url": "https://startupwired.com/2026/01/05/top-startups-founded-by-ex-google-employees-shaping-tech/", "snippet": "[Jina Parallel Content] Title: Top Startups Founded by Ex Google Employees Shaping Tech URL Source: https://startupwired.com/2026/01/05/top-startups-founded-by-ex-google-employees-shaping-tech/ Published Time: 2026-01-05T18:00:00+05:30 Markdown Content: Google does more than build products; it trains founders. Engineers, researchers, and executives who leave Google often launch startups that redefine entire markets. Venture capital firms track this \u201cXoogler\u201d effect closely because former Google employees bring scale-first thinking, deep AI expertise, and strong execution discipline. In 2024 and 2025, startups founded or led by ex-Google talent continued to dominate funding rounds, talent wars, and enterprise adoption, especially in artificial intelligence. Below is a detailed look at the most influential startups founded by former Google employees, along with the latest developments and strategic momentum. ## Glean \u2013 Reinventing enterprise search with AI Arvind Jain spent more than a decade at [Google](https://www.google.com/), where he helped build Search, Maps, and YouTube infrastructure. He founded Glean to fix a problem he understood deeply: employees waste hours searching across fragmented internal tools. Glean delivers an AI-powered enterprise search and assistant that understands company context. The platform connects to Slack, Google Workspace, Jira, Confluence, and dozens of SaaS tools, then delivers precise answers instead of keyword lists. Large enterprises adopted Glean quickly because the product reduced onboarding time and improved productivity. In late 2024, Glean raised a major growth round that pushed its valuation beyond $4 billion. The company expanded aggressively across North America and Europe and hired senior enterprise sales leaders to support Fortune 500 customers. Glean now competes directly with Microsoft and emerging AI assistant platforms, yet it maintains an edge through deep search relevance expertise rooted in Google DNA. ## Character.AI \u2013 Consumer AI built by model pioneers Noam Shazeer and Daniel de Freitas left Google after contributing to foundational transformer research. They launched Character.AI to explore conversational AI with personality, memory, and emotional tone. Users can talk with fictional characters, historical figures, or custom-created personas. Character.AI achieved explosive consumer growth and ranked among the fastest-growing AI apps globally. In 2024, the company entered a high-profile agreement with Google that combined technology licensing and strategic collaboration. That move highlighted a broader trend: Big Tech increasingly partners with startups founded by its own alumni to regain momentum in competitive AI races. Character.AI continues to invest in safety tooling, creator monetization, and long-form conversational memory. The company also explores subscription revenue as consumer AI demand matures. ## Cohere \u2013 Private and secure LLMs for enterprises Aidan Gomez, one of the authors behind the original transf", "relevance_score": 0.85}, {"title": "From Google to game changers: 9 startups founded by ex-Googlers that ...", "url": "https://techfundingnews.com/ex-googlers-take-the-lead-startups-that-raised-funding-to-power-ai-boom/", "snippet": "[Jina Parallel Content] Title: From Google to game changers: 9 startups founded by ex-Googlers that secured big funding in AI URL Source: https://techfundingnews.com/ex-googlers-take-the-lead-startups-that-raised-funding-to-power-ai-boom/ Published Time: 2024-12-20T08:00:52+00:00 Markdown Content: # From Google to game changers: 9 startups founded by ex-Googlers that secured big funding in AI \u2014 TFN We value your privacy We and our 1714 partners use cookies and other tracking technologies to improve your experience on our website. Additionally, we may utilize precise geolocation data and identification through device scanning. Please note that your choices will apply only to this website. Once you give consent, a floating button will appear at the bottom of your screen, allowing you to change or withdraw your consent at any time. We respect your choices and are committed to providing you with a transparent and secure browsing experience. You will find detailed information about all cookies under each consent category below.\n...\nTitle: From Google to game changers: 9 startups founded by ex-Googlers that secured big funding in AI URL Source: https://techfundingnews.com/ex-googlers-take-the-lead-startups-that-raised-funding-to-power-ai-boom/ Published Time: 2024-12-20T08:00:52+00:00 Markdown Content: # From Google to game changers: 9 startups founded by ex-Googlers that secured big funding in AI \u2014 TFN We value your privacy We and our 1714 partners use cookies and other tracking technologies to improve your experience on our website. We may store and/or access information on a device and process personal data, such as your IP address and browsing data, for personalised advertising and content, advertising and content measurement, audience research and services development. Additionally, we may utilize precise geolocation data and identification through device scanning", "relevance_score": 0.85}, {"title": "The Google Mafia Map: 1,200+ startups founded by ex-Googlers - CB Insights", "url": "https://www.cbinsights.com/research/google-mafia-map/", "snippet": "[Jina Parallel Content] Title: The Google Mafia Map: 1,200+ startups founded by ex-Googlers \u2014 and the 10 you should know about URL Source: https://www.cbinsights.com/research/google-mafia-map/ Published Time: 2023/12/08 Markdown Content: In 2017, 8 [Google](https://www.cbinsights.com/investor/google) researchers published a paper introducing a [new neural network architecture called the \u201cTransformer\u201d](https://www.cbinsights.com/research/google-transformer-startups-openai/) that could better understand and generate human language. The Transformer paper was a major breakthrough in the field of natural language processing, and it helped to enable more advanced language models, like the ones being developed by [OpenAI](https://www.cbinsights.com/company/openai). Most of the researchers behind this paper have gone on to found companies and raise significant financing, often at large valuations. For example, members of this group have founded genAI unicorns [Cohere](https://www.cbinsights.com/company/cohere-3), [Character.AI](https://www.cbinsights.com/company/characterai), and [Adept](https://www.cbinsights.com/company/adept-3). Other impressive former Google employees \u2014 i.e., members of its alumni network, or \u201cmafia\u201d \u2014 are founding companies in areas ranging from fintech to entertainment tech to workplace automation. Highlights from the Google Mafia include: *   **1,231 companies have been founded by ex-Google employees since 2018****:** These companies have raised a combined $22B in total disclosed equity funding. More than 800 of them have received venture capital backing, recording an average total funding of $26M and a median total funding of $5M. *   **21 Google mafia-founded startups have reached unicorn status in the last 5 years:**Startups that have joined the $1B+ valuation club include Adept and [Inflection AI](https://www.cbinsights.com/company/inflection-ai). *   **58 startups founded by former Google employees have exited since 2018:** Of these startups, 56 have been acquired. *   **Andreessen Horowitz is the Google mafia\u2019s most active VC investor:** The VC has invested in 46 Google alum-founded companies since 2018, including Character.AI and [Mem](https://www.cbinsights.com/company/mem-1). Alumni Ventures and Soma Capital follow close behind, as they have invested in 36 and 35 Google mafia-founded companies, respectively. Using the CB Insights Mosaic algorithm, we evaluated the management team strength of Google alum-founded companies to identify 10 key early-stage players. [![Image 1: The Google Mafia Map: 10 Google alum-founded early-stage startups with impressive management teams](https://research-assets.cbinsights.com/2023/12/05151110/GoogleMafia-Top10-122023-2-987x1024.png)](https://research-assets.cbinsights.com/2023/12/05151110/GoogleMafia-Top10-122023-2.png) _This list consists of VC-backed, early-stage (seed and Series A) companies founded by former Google leaders. All companies on the list have raised funding since 2022. They are listed in descendin", "relevance_score": 0.85}]
